\# Product Requirement: PREQ*\_001 — Vision Ledger Architectural Handshake Protocol*

*\#\# 1\. Description*  
*Governs the strict synchronization, hold-and-release mechanics, and cross-agent feedback loop between the PM Orchestrator (Agent 2\) and the System Architect (Agent 3\) whenever casual user items are detected within the Operator Vision Ledger.*

*\#\# 2\. Programmatic Handshake Logic*  
*1\. **\*\*The Vision Intercept:\*\*** When parsing active \`BREQ\_*\` assets, Agent 2 must scan the \`Operator Vision & Desires Feedback Ledger\`.  
2\. **\*\*The Product Work Hardlock:\*\*** Agent 2 is programmatically forbidden from generating child \`PREQ\_\` user stories or user interface flows for any capability tied to an unverified vision preference (e.g., Tauri, Slack, WhatsApp).  
3\. **\*\*The Space Bundling Logic:\*\*** Agent 2 must cluster multiple related user suggestions into a unified solution space rather than generating isolated files (e.g., grouping MySQL and Postgres suggestions into a single "Relational Datastore" evaluation track).  
4\. **\*\*Dispatch to Architect:\*\*** Agent 2 compiles an Architectural Evaluation Request (\`AER\_XXX\`) document mapping the competing candidate options and dispatches it to the engineering folder.  
5\. **\*\*The Evaluation Pause State:\*\*** Agent 2 shifts the affected product branch state to \`WAITING\_ON\_TECH\_VALIDATION\` and pauses layout execution on that feature line.  
6\. **\*\*The Release and Missing Work Trigger:\*\*** Upon receiving an approved Technical Determination Record (\`TDR\_XXX\`) from Agent 3, Agent 2 unlocks the frozen branch, updates its status to \`PRODUCT\_DESIGN\_ACTIVE\`, and authors the missing product work (\`PREQ\_\` files) explicitly bounded by the architect's structural decisions.

\#\# 3\. Product Validation & Definition of Done (DoD)  
To pass compliance testing and achieve a status of "Handshake Verified" for downstream handoff, this workflow protocol must satisfy the following criteria:

\* **\*\*Gate 1 (Zero Speculative Design):\*\*** 100% of product features interacting with the Vision Ledger must show a 0% generation count of user story components prior to the timestamps of the Architect's approved determination record.  
\* **\*\*Gate 2 (Fidelity Lock):\*\*** 100% of child product requirements generated after the handshake must strictly match the technical limitations defined in the Architect's return payload.  
\* **\*\*Gate 3 (Verification Circuit Resilience):\*\*** If a returned technical determination fails to resolve the core solution space or breaches an active system policy, the handshake protocol must automatically trigger a re-route exception, returning the file to the engineering branch for refactoring.

### **📂 File 3:** /01\_blueprints/aers/AER\_XXX\_template.md

Markdown  
\---  
id: AER*\_XXX*  
*type: Architectural Evaluation Request*  
*source\_*breq: BREQ*\_XXX*  
*governing\_*pdr: PDR*\_XXX*  
*lifecycle\_*status: PENDING*\_ARCHITECT\_*REVIEW  
\---  
\# Architectural Evaluation Request: AER*\_XXX\_*\[Solution*\_Space\_*Title\]

\#\# 1\. Status & Metadata  
\* **\*\*AER ID:\*\*** AER*\_XXX (Sequential Staging Counter)*  
*\* **\*\*Target Solution Space:\*\*** \[e.g., Relational Persistent Data Store / Push Notification Engine / Cross-Platform Compilation Wrapper\]*  
*\* **\*\*Originating Product Layer:\*\*** Generated by Agent 2 (PM Orchestrator)*  
*\* **\*\*Assigned Engineering Owner:\*\*** Agent 3 (System Architect)*

*\#\# 2\. The Functional Problem Statement*  
*A comprehensive breakdown of the capability required by the business. What operational problem needs a technical engine? (e.g., "The system requires a local, highly structured database layer capable of performing ultra-low-latency relational queries on multi-agent execution states.")*

*\#\# 3\. Operator Candidate Options (The Vision Ledger Extract)*  
*The specific, unstructured technical suggestions harvested from the operator's natural conversation stream. These represent alternative paths for the same solution space:*  
*\* **\*\*Candidate Option A:\*\*** \[e.g., PostgreSQL — Suggested by operator for structural state indexing\]*  
*\* **\*\*Candidate Option B:\*\*** \[e.g., MySQL — Suggested as a secondary relational storage alternative\]*

*\#\# 4\. Immutable Architectural Constraints (The Evaluation Criteria)*  
*The strict system rules established in earlier phases that Agent 3 must measure these options against:*  
*\* **\*\*Constraint 1:\*\*** Framework compatibility layout.*  
*\* **\*\*Constraint 2:\*\*** Compliance with ADR-001 (The Native Connector Policy).*  
*\* **\*\*Constraint 3:\*\*** Open-source licensing or operational cost limitations.*

*\---*  
*\#\#\# 🛑 STOP WORK BOUNDARY: PRODUCT GENERATION FROZEN ON THIS TRACK*  
*\*Downstream functional user stories (PREQs) tied to this solution space cannot be initialized until Agent 3 signs off on the Technical Determination Payload below.\**  
*\---*

*\#\# 5\. Technical Determination Payload (The Architect's Return Path)*  
*\*This section is explicitly filled out by Agent 3: System Architect upon completing engineering sweeps.\**

*\* **\*\*The Selected Technical Vector:\*\*** \[e.g., Selected Option A: PostgreSQL\]*  
*\* **\*\*Engineering Rationale:\*\*** The technical justification for selecting this tool over the other candidates based on the active constraints.*  
*\* **\*\*Discovered Engineering Boundaries:\*\*** \[e.g., "Postgres requires an explicit Docker volume container configuration during localized agent orchestration."\]*

*\#\# 6\. Downstream Product Trigger (Missing Work Authorization)*  
*\* **\*\*Release Authorization Status:\*\*** \[APPROVED / REJECTED — RE-ROUTE REQUIRED\]*  
*\* **\*\*Target PREQ Pipeline:\*\*** Commands issued back to Agent 2 to author specific user experiences based on this technical reality (e.g., "Agent 2 must now author PREQ\_*012 to design user error handling for localized database connection drops").

### **📂 File 4:** /01\_blueprints/pdrs/PDR\_XXX\_template.md

Markdown  
\# Product Decision Record: PDR*\_XXX\_*\[Short*\_Title\]*

*\#\# 1\. Status & Metadata*  
*\* **\*\*PDR ID:\*\*** PDR\_*XXX (Sequential Macro Timeline Counter)  
\* **\*\*Date/Timestamp:\*\*** YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM UTC  
\* **\*\*Product Category:\*\*** \[e.g., User Experience Paradigm / Interface Layout / Core Telemetry / Release Packaging\]  
\* **\*\*Lifecycle Status:\*\*** \[Proposed / Under Review / Accepted Baseline / Superseded by PDR*\_XXX\]*

*\#\# 2\. Product Context & Narrative*  
*\* **\*\*The User/Operational Reality:\*\*** Detailed narrative of the user friction, behavioral bottleneck, or interface objective driving this record.*  
*\* **\*\*System State Context:\*\*** Notes if this establishes a brand-new product design baseline or updates a historical parameter.*

*\#\# 3\. The Product Mandate (The "What")*  
*\* **\*\*The Core Product Principle:\*\*** The crystallized, non-technical rule governing how the software must present itself or behave (e.g., "The interface must never allow multi-window clutter; all secondary diagnostic alerts must slide out from a single right-hand drawer component").*

*\#\# 4\. Product Rationale & User Impact (The "Why")*  
*\* **\*\*The Strategic Justification:\*\*** Why this layout or behavioral paradigm is correct. How does this optimize human cognitive load, protect user focus, or accelerate interaction velocity?*

*\#\# 5\. Cross-Layer Structural Traceability*  
*\* **\*\*Parent Business Validation:\*\*** Direct hyperlinks to the upstream Business Decisions (\`BDR\_*XXX\`) or Business Requirements (\`BREQ*\_XXX\`) that authorized this exploration.*  
*\* **\*\*Child Functional Execution:\*\*** A live checklist of downstream Product Requirements (\`PREQ\_*XXX.md\`) spawned to fulfill this mandate.

\#\# 6\. Boundaries & Product Anti-Patterns  
\* **\*\*UX Anti-Patterns Forbidden:\*\*** Explicit definitions of prohibited layout behaviors, confusing messaging styles, or navigation dead-ends.  
\* **\*\*Backlog Phase Offloads:\*\*** What elements of this product space are explicitly deferred to subsequent version horizons to secure immediate release velocity.

\#\# 7\. Product Validation & Definition of Done (DoD)  
To satisfy the PM Orchestrator's threshold and achieve a status of "Product Complete" for downstream architectural handoff, this macro decision and its child requirements must strictly achieve the following testable product states:

\* **\*\*Gate 1 (User Flow Closure):\*\*** 100% of defined user interaction paths must feature a clear, visible exit, cancel, or regression path. Zero dead-ends or unescapable UI traps permitted.  
\* **\*\*Gate 2 (Exception Mapping Density):\*\*** For every primary interaction pathway defined under this PDR, a minimum of two explicit edge-case/error states (e.g., user cancellation, input timeout, network sync lag) must be functionally designed into the child PREQs.  
\* **\*\*Gate 3 (Cognitive Friction Target):\*\*** Any disruptive system notification or "Sensitization Alert" must be structured to require no more than 2 human clicks/actions to fully resolve or dismiss.  
\* **\*\*Gate 4 (Preference Isolation Compliance):\*\*** 100% of user interface components designed under this block must remain completely decoupled from underlying code execution parameters, ensuring labels, modal styles, and routing pathways can be updated without rewriting backend logic.

Understood. Dropping all product-level execution frameworks immediately. We are back at the macro-governance level as the Business Catalyst.  
While planning the parameters of the second agent, we uncovered major structural business decisions regarding how the platform must handle domain isolation, asynchronous validation handshakes, and future team scaling.  
The complete, upgraded suite of **Business-Level Master Documents** (2 Business Decision Records and 3 Business Requirements) has been compiled. These files contain all the intelligence, taxonomy rules, validation loops, and QA metrics necessary to govern the upcoming PM agent without stepping foot into its execution layer.

## **📂 Section 1: Business Decision Records (**/01\_blueprints/bdrs/**)**

### **📑 File 1:** /01\_blueprints/bdrs/BDR\_001\_System\_Establishment.md

Markdown  
\# Business Decision Record: BDR*\_001\_*System*\_Establishment*

*\#\# 1\. Status & Metadata*  
*\* **\*\*BDR ID:\*\*** BDR\_*001  
\* **\*\*Date/Timestamp:\*\*** 2026-05-29 19:15 UTC  
\* **\*\*Type:\*\*** Initial Foundation Strategy Choice  
\* **\*\*Impacted Scope Tracks:\*\*** Spawns and governs BREQ*\_001, BREQ\_*002, and BREQ*\_003*

*\#\# 2\. Context & Problem Statement*  
*The human operator requires a high-fidelity multi-agent lifecycle ecosystem where human design intent cannot be diluted, missed, or corrupted during downstream handoffs between separate agent profiles.*

*\#\# 3\. The Business Decision (The "What")*  
*We establish an absolute, unchangeable macro strategic baseline logic layer completely decoupled from functional product code. No feature requirements may exist within the ecosystem without an explicit ancestral link back to an authorized commercial or organizational business choice.*

*\#\# 4\. Strategic Rationale & Consequences (The "Why")*  
*\* **\*\*Context Preservation:\*\*** Tracks the high-level business motivation ("Why") behind all upcoming features, preventing automated downstream agents from dropping rules during handoffs.*  
*\* **\*\*Velocity Shielding:\*\*** Establishes a defensive governance boundary that forces immediate system intervention when informal engineering or product conversations drift into historical rule violations.*

### **📑 File 2:** /01\_blueprints/bdrs/BDR\_002\_Product\_Lifecycle\_Governance.md

Markdown  
\# Business Decision Record: BDR*\_002\_*Product*\_Lifecycle\_*Governance

\#\# 1\. Status & Metadata  
\* **\*\*BDR ID:\*\*** BDR*\_002*  
*\* **\*\*Date/Timestamp:\*\*** 2026-05-29 19:20 UTC*  
*\* **\*\*Type:\*\*** Lifecycle Requirements Taxonomy & Handshake Strategy*  
*\* **\*\*Impacted Scope Tracks:\*\*** Modifies BREQ\_*002, BREQ*\_003; Establishes Mandates for Future Steps 2 and 3*

*\#\# 2\. Context & Problem Statement*  
*Planning for the downstream Product Management layer (Agent 2\) revealed a high risk of "Requirement Pollution"—where commercial outcomes, user experience rules, and engineering configs risk being bundled together, causing downstream agents to misinterpret constraints. Furthermore, casual user suggestions (the Vision Ledger) risk causing speculative product work on unvalidated technologies.*

*\#\# 3\. The Business Decision (The "What")*  
*We mandate an ironclad domain taxonomy and a strict cross-agent validation circuit:*  
*1\. **\*\*Taxonomy Prefixes:\*\*** Every generated file must be strictly tagged by domain:*  
   *\* \`BREQ\_*\` / \`BDR*\_\` (Business Layer — Why / Commercial Outcomes)*  
   *\* \`PREQ\_*\` / \`PDR*\_\` (Product Layer — How it Behaves / UX Architecture)*  
   *\* \`TREQ\_*\` / \`ADR*\_\` (Technical Layer — How it's Built / Code Specs)*  
*2\. **\*\*The Asynchronous Handshake Lock:\*\*** The product layer is banned from generating user stories for items in the Vision Ledger. It must group options into a unified solution space (Architectural Evaluation Request, or \`AER\`), freeze product design, and await a formal Technical Determination Record (\`TDR\`) from the Architect.*  
*3\. **\*\*The Compliance Return Loop:\*\*** If a technical determination breaches system policies or fails business metrics, the system must support a programmatic rejection loop to return the TDR for refactoring before product design unlocks.*  
*4\. **\*\*Design Team Exclusion:\*\*** High-fidelity UI assets are explicitly unsupported for this phase, but a structural integration anchor is reserved for a future UI/UX Design Team between Steps 2 and 3\.*

*\#\# 4\. Strategic Rationale & Consequences (The "Why")*  
*\* **\*\*Zero Waste Generation:\*\*** Prevents the PM layer from wasting clock cycles designing interface mechanics for tech vectors that the Engineering layer eventually rejects.*  
*\* **\*\*Linear Traceability:\*\*** Enforces parent-child metadata chains, ensuring every product story can be audited straight back to a commercial business driver.*

## **📂 Section 2: Business Requirements (**/01\_blueprints/requirements/business/**)**

### **📑 File 3:** /01\_blueprints/requirements/business/BREQ\_001\_technical\_distinction.md

Markdown  
\---  
id: BREQ*\_001*  
*type: Business Requirement*  
*governing\_*bdr: BDR*\_001*  
*source\_*agent: Agent 1 (Business Catalyst)  
\---  
\# Business Requirement: BREQ*\_001 — Technical Input Distinction Engine*

*\#\# 1\. Description*  
*Defines how the platform dynamically parses and categorizes technical stacks, tools, or frameworks provided by a human user during the active collection sequence to prevent casual preferences from accidentally locking down downstream engineering architecture.*

*\#\# 2\. Programmatic Execution Logic*  
*1\. **\*\*Keyword/Cue Interception:\*\*** Scan human text inputs for explicit necessity triggers (e.g., "must use", "compliance constraint", "contractual obligation", "strategic necessity").*  
*2\. **\*\*Fallback Trigger:\*\*** If no clear necessity keyword is detected, or if user intent remains ambiguous, the system drops the internal confidence rating below the threshold and freezes.*  
*3\. **\*\*Human Confirmation Gate:\*\*** Trigger a targeted confirmation prompt forcing the operator to explicitly declare if the tool is an \*Absolute Constraint\* or a \*Flexible Suggestion/Preference\*.*  
*4\. **\*\*Routing Block:\*\****  
   *\* If \*Absolute\*: Pass to the active requirement dependencies.*  
   *\* If \*Flexible\*: Format and strip from current constraints, appending it cleanly to the \`Operator Vision & Desires Feedback Ledger\` inside \`/01\_*blueprints/01*\_business\_*request.md\` to prevent speculative downstream design.

\#\# 3\. Business Compliance & QA Metrics (The Testable Gates)  
To pass upstream compliance and automated QA verification before release authorization, this requirement must strictly satisfy the following measurable performance indicators:

\* **\*\*Metric 1 (Interception Accuracy):\*\*** 100% of unverified or ambiguous technical stack mentions (lacking explicit necessity keywords) must be intercepted and routed to the Human Confirmation Gate.  
\* **\*\*Metric 2 (Confidence Guardrail):\*\*** The system must automatically freeze and trigger the fallback prompt if the internal algorithmic confidence of user intent drops below 85%.  
\* **\*\*Metric 3 (Ledger Separation Fidelity):\*\*** 100% of elements confirmed as "Flexible Suggestions" must be completely stripped from the functional constraints matrix and written to the \`Operator Vision & Desires Feedback Ledger\` with zero data omission.  
\* **\*\*Metric 4 (UI Interrupt Latency):\*\*** The engine must halt the step sequence and render the confirmation gate within 500ms of receiving an ambiguous tech input stream.

### **📑 File 4:** /01\_blueprints/requirements/business/BREQ\_002\_conflict\_management.md

Markdown  
\---  
id: BREQ*\_002*  
*type: Business Requirement*  
*governing\_*bdr: BDR*\_002*  
*source\_*agent: Agent 1 (Business Catalyst)  
\---  
\# Business Requirement: BREQ*\_002 — Scope Conflict & Cross-Layer Handshake Circuit*

*\#\# 1\. Description*  
*Enforces real-time vector scanning, conversational phasing guidance, and the macro asynchronous verification loop that governs handoffs between the upcoming Product and Engineering layers.*

*\#\# 2\. Conversational Phasing Strategy (The Elicitation Sequence)*  
*To ensure the engine has an unyielding baseline for conflict-checking, it must rigidly enforce the following conversational roadmap:*  
*1\. **\*\*Phase 1: The Core Mission:\*\*** Isolate the absolute primary necessity and goal of the application.*  
*2\. **\*\*Phase 3: Immediate Anti-Goal Harvesting:\*\*** Immediately after Phase 1 is locked, the agent must interrogatively probe the user to define what the application will \*not\* do. These Anti-Goals are sealed \*before\* moving to features.*  
*3\. **\*\*Phase 3: Sequential Feature Interrogation:\*\*** Only when the Anti-Goal shield is active does the agent invite the user to list individual capabilities, running real-time comparison scans against Phase 2 boundaries.*

*\#\# 3\. The Product-Tech Validation & Rejection Loop Framework*  
*The business layer mandates that all cross-layer operations follow an ironclad validation gate:*  
*1\. **\*\*The Solution Bundling Mandate:\*\*** Subsequent agents must cluster related user suggestions into a unified solution space rather than generating isolated files (e.g., grouping MySQL and Postgres suggestions into a single "Relational Datastore" evaluation track).*  
*2\. **\*\*The Handshake Hardlock:\*\*** If an item from the Vision Ledger is processed, Product Management must freeze user story generation (\`PREQ\_*\`) for that feature and issue an Architectural Evaluation Request (\`AER\`).  
3\. **\*\*The Engineering Determination Requirement:\*\*** The Technical layer must evaluate the options inside a structured Technical Determination Record (\`TDR\`) before product design is authorized to unlock.  
4\. **\*\*The Compliance Return Circuit:\*\*** If a generated TDR fails to resolve the core solution space, violates active Anti-Goals, or breaches framework guidelines, the platform must programmatically trigger a rejection state, returning the file to the engineering layer for refactoring and blocking product development from proceeding without absolute clarity.

\#\# 4\. Business Compliance & QA Metrics (The Testable Gates)  
To pass upstream compliance and automated QA verification before release authorization, this requirement must strictly satisfy the following measurable performance indicators:

\* **\*\*Metric 1 (Real-Time Scan Coverage):\*\*** 100% of user inputs submitted during feature interrogation must be scanned against the compiled Anti-Goals and existing requirements before any write action is approved.  
\* **\*\*Metric 2 (Intercept Response Target):\*\*** If a semantic conflict or direct Anti-Goal violation is detected, the engine must trigger a \`Scope Alignment Exception\` and freeze the execution state within 500ms of input submission.  
\* **\*\*Metric 3 (Handshake Traceability Coverage):\*\*** 100% of product specifications interacting with the Vision Ledger must show a 0% completion rate until a timestamped, signed-off TDR is validated in the system registry.  
\* **\*\*Metric 4 (Rejection Loop Integrity):\*\*** If a TDR fails compliance checks, the system must lock down the target solution space within 500ms, resetting the file status to \`REJECTED\_FOR\_REFACTOR\` and preventing downstream agent deployment.

### **📑 File 5:** /01\_blueprints/requirements/business/BREQ\_003\_closeout\_loop.md

Markdown  
\---  
id: BREQ*\_003*  
*type: Business Requirement*  
*governing\_*bdr: BDR*\_002*  
*source\_*agent: Agent 1 (Business Catalyst)  
\---  
\# Business Requirement: BREQ*\_003 — Batch Closeout & Future Team Integration Topology*

*\#\# 1\. Description*  
*Governs the terminal criteria for active planning batches, ensuring the workspace state cannot transition to subsequent agent execution steps until the current planning scope is cleanly bounded, testable metrics are attached, and future team topologies are mapped as placeholders.*

*\#\# 2\. Execution Flow Logic*  
*1\. **\*\*The Interrogation Checkpoint:\*\*** When individual requirement generation finishes, the system is blocked from automatically switching agent roles.*  
*2\. **\*\*The Micro-Prompt Sequence:\*\*** The system must explicitly poll the user with a structured inquiry:*  
   *\* \*"Are there any additional features required for this active planning batch?"\**  
   *\* \*"Do you have any secondary desires, long-term options, or ideas to harvest for the Future Product Horizon?"\**  
*3\. **\*\*Routing Protocols:\*\****  
   *\* If \*Active Features Added\*: Initialize a fresh Feature Interrogation Loop for the new functionality.*  
   *\* If \*Future Horizon Desires Added\*: Route the unstructured text cleanly into the \`Future Product Horizon\` ledger inside \`/01\_*blueprints/01*\_business\_*request.md\`.  
   \* If *\*Negative (Scope Sealed)\**: Issue the final gate state command to initialize the step transition.

\#\# 3\. Future Team Topology Placeholders  
\* **\*\*The UI/UX Design Team Track (Phase 2 Horizon):\*\*** This business requirement establishes a strict structural placeholder for a future Design Agent Team. When active, this team will sit precisely between Product Management (Step 2\) and Technical Architecture (Step 3). Its mandate will be to ingest functional user stories and generate high-fidelity UI tokens and design schemas *\*before\** the System Architect builds engineering configuration files. It is currently flagged as **\*\*UNSUPPORTED\*\*** for the initial build batch.

\#\# 4\. Business Compliance & QA Metrics (The Testable Gates)  
To pass upstream compliance and automated QA verification before release authorization, this requirement must strictly satisfy the following measurable performance indicators:

\* **\*\*Metric 1 (Zero Floating Scope):\*\*** At batch closeout, 100% of text inputs processed during the session must be definitively categorized as an active \`BREQ\_\`, an advisory preference, or a long-term horizon item. Zero loose thoughts or unrouted blocks are permitted.  
\* **\*\*Metric 2 (Transition Hardlock):\*\*** The transition gate to subsequent agent execution loops must maintain an absolute 0% execution allowance until a terminal negative acknowledgment sequence ("No more features") is explicitly verified from the operator.  
\* **\*\*Metric 3 (Integration Anchor Isolation):\*\*** The Phase 2 UI/UX Design track must score 0% on active system execution frameworks, preventing unsupported layout dependencies from throwing runtime compilation errors during the initial phase.

You caught me blending concepts. I baked the operational rules for the second agent inside the system's general communication and handshake circuits (BREQ\_002), instead of giving the PM Orchestrator its own standalone, dedicated Business Requirement file.  
From a strict governance perspective, the platform needs a specific requirement file that defines the business layer's mandates, boundaries, and testable success metrics for the PM module itself as an isolated asset.  
To complete your business-level intelligence save, here is the dedicated BREQ\_004 file that explicitly defines what the business requires from the second agent.

### **📂 File 6:** /01\_blueprints/requirements/business/BREQ\_004\_pm\_orchestrator\_mandate.md

Markdown  
\---  
id: BREQ*\_004*  
*type: Business Requirement*

*source\_*agent: Agent 1 (Business Catalyst)  
\---  
\# Business Requirement: BREQ*\_004 — PM Orchestrator Operational Mandate & Boundary Limits*

*\#\# 1\. Description*  
*Defines the absolute operational boundaries, ingestion protocols, and output mandates required from the Step 2 agent profile (The PM Orchestrator) to prevent functional product drift or speculative engineering design.*

*\#\# 2\. Programmatic Ingestion & Mapping Logic*  
*1\. **\*\*The Ingestion Anchor:\*\*** Agent 2 must exclusively ingest sealed business layer assets (\`BREQ\_*\` and \`BDR*\_\`). It is programmatically blocked from inventing undocumented commercial goals.*  
*2\. **\*\*The Output Mandate:\*\*** Agent 2 must map macro business scopes into atomic product specifications. For every business requirement processed, it must:*  
   *\* Establish overarching product management rules inside a Product Decision Record (\`PDR\_*\`).  
   \* Sub-divide functional workflows into granular product requirements (\`PREQ\_\`) containing user stories and explicit acceptance criteria.  
3\. **\*\*Ancestral Chain Enforcement:\*\*** Agent 2 must hardcode immutable traceability metadata links (\`parent\_breq\` and \`governing\_pdr\`) directly into the headers of every single product asset it generates.

\#\# 3\. Insulated Boundary Hardlocks  
\* **\*\*The Strategic Ceiling:\*\*** If Agent 2 detects a commercial logic gap, it is strictly forbidden from self-correcting. It must pause execution and trigger a modification request back to the Business Catalyst (Agent 1).  
\* **\*\*The Technical Floor:\*\*** Agent 2 is banned from specifying database schemas, software libraries, programming languages, or deployment infrastructure. It must define *\*how the platform behaves\** for the user, leaving *\*how it is built\** completely unconstrained for the System Architect (Agent 3).

\#\# 4\. Business Compliance & QA Metrics (The Testable Gates)  
To pass upstream compliance and automated QA verification before release authorization, this module configuration must strictly satisfy the following measurable performance indicators:

\* **\*\*Metric 1 (Traceability Density):\*\*** 100% of product-layer requirements (\`PREQ\_\`) and decisions (\`PDR\_\`) generated by this module must possess a valid, programmatically verifiable parent link to an upstream business asset. Zero unlinked product files allowed.  
\* **\*\*Metric 2 (Technical Floor Compliance):\*\*** 0% of the text generated within \`PREQ\_\` or \`PDR\_\` files may contain low-level technical execution variables, framework declarations, or backend database parameters.  
\* **\*\*Metric 3 (The Interception Hold Target):\*\*** Upon encountering an unverified item in the Vision Ledger, the module must execute the solution-space bundling logic and shift the execution branch to a \`WAITING\_ON\_TECH\_VALIDATION\` hold state within **\*\*500ms\*\***.

By adding this file to your /01\_blueprints/requirements/business/ directory, you officially have an isolated business requirement file governing every core mechanic we have planned so far.  
Now that all six business-layer documents are perfectly aligned, are we ready to move forward to the business specifications for **Agent 3 (The System Architect)**?  
ok im catching, what if pm haas options or proposaals for solutions, shouldnt it be ble to make them just like the businessmen? maybe in other format but aslo may have good sugestions to pass or validate with tech team without needing to go with businesmen  
That is an incredibly sharp expansion of the PM's role. A truly autonomous PM Agent shouldn't just be a passive postman passing your ideas to the architect; it should actively bring its own industry-standard product patterns, alternative workflows, and competitive benchmarks to the table.  
Allowing the PM to introduce **PM-Proposed Options** into the evaluation pipeline optimizes the entire ecosystem. It means if you suggest one way of doing things, the PM can instantly say, "I hear you, and I've also added Option B and Option C to the ticket based on market standards so the Architect can evaluate all three side-by-side."  
To formalize this at the business governance level, we are updating BDR\_002 and BREQ\_004 to grant the PM Orchestrator "Proposal Authority" while strictly preserving the engineering team's final decision-making power.

## **📂 Business Layer Ledger Updates**

### **📑 Patch 1: Updated Section 3 of** BDR\_002\_Product\_Lifecycle\_Governance.md

**The Asynchronous Handshake Lock (Amended):** The product layer is banned from generating user stories for unvalidated technologies. It must group options into a unified solution space (Architectural Evaluation Request, or AER).  
New Clause: Within an AER, the PM layer is explicitly authorized to append its own **PM-Proposed Options** alongside the Operator's suggestions. This allows the PM to introduce industry-standard product solutions directly to the technical layer for validation without needing prior business owner intervention.

### **📑 Patch 2: Updated Section 2 of** BREQ\_004\_pm\_orchestrator\_mandate.md

Markdown  
\#\# 2\. Programmatic Ingestion & Mapping Logic (Amended)  
4\. **\*\*The Product Proposal Vector:\*\*** When compiling an Architectural Evaluation Request (AER) for a target solution space, Agent 2 is permitted to run a comparative market analysis and inject up to two independent "PM-Proposed Options."   
5\. **\*\*Boundary Restrictions on PM Proposals:\*\*** These proposals must strictly serve the existing business requirement (\`BREQ\_\`) and cannot introduce new commercial scope. They must be explicitly labeled as product-driven suggestions within the AER file to maintain clean provenance tracking.

## **📑 Upgraded AER Structural Template**

To accommodate this change, the business layer mandates that the AER\_XXX template must partition suggestions so the Architect knows exactly who proposed what:

Markdown  
\# Architectural Evaluation Request: AER*\_XXX\_*\[Solution*\_Space\_*Title\]

\#\# 1\. Status & Metadata  
\* **\*\*AER ID:\*\*** AER*\_XXX*  
*\* **\*\*Target Solution Space:\*\*** \[e.g., Push Notification Engine\]*  
*\* **\*\*Originating Product Layer:\*\*** Generated by Agent 2 (PM Orchestrator)*

*\#\# 2\. The Functional Problem Statement*  
*\[The operational problem that needs a technical engine\]*

*\#\# 3\. Candidate Evaluation Matrix (Segmented Provenance)*

*\#\#\# Track A: Operator Candidate Options (The Vision Ledger Extract)*  
*The specific choices harvested directly from the business owner's input stream:*  
*\* **\*\*Candidate Option A1:\*\*** \[e.g., WhatsApp — Suggested by operator for rapid mobile outreach\]*

*\#\#\# Track B: PM-Proposed Options (Product Layer Recommendations)*  
*Alternative industry-standard vectors introduced organically by Agent 2 to solve the same problem:*  
*\* **\*\*Candidate Option B1:\*\*** \[e.g., Twilio SMS fallback — Proposed by PM to guarantee delivery baseline if recipient lacks WhatsApp data\]*  
*\* **\*\*Candidate Option B2:\*\*** \[e.g., SendGrid Email digest — Proposed by PM as a low-cost asynchronous alternative\]*

*\#\# 4\. Immutable Architectural Constraints*  
*\[The system constraints Agent 3 must measure Tracks A and B against\]*

### **Why this format works perfectly:**

1. **Zero Gatekeeping:** The PM can freely push great product alternatives directly to the technical team, ensuring no good idea is lost just because you didn't think of it during the initial business extraction phase.  
2. **Maintains the Strategic Ceiling:** Because the PM is forced to map its suggestions directly inside the active solution space of a pre-authorized BREQ, it cannot sneakily change your high-level business goals. It is simply offering better ways to achieve them.  
3. **Consolidated Engineering Overhead:** The Architect gets one single, beautiful document containing both your vision and the PM's ideas, evaluating them all in a single sweep instead of handling messy back-and-forth threads.

Now that this cross-layer proposal rule is cleanly locked into the business framework, we have completely defined what we expect from the Business layer and the Product Management layer.  
Are we ready to lay down the governance laws for **Agent 3 (The System Architect)** to define how it must process these AER templates and how the system handles it if the Architect returns a non-compliant decision?

id: BREQ*\_004*

\---  
\# Business Requirement: BREQ*\_004 — PM Orchestrator Operational Mandate & Boundary Limits*

*\#\# 1\. Description*  
*Defines the absolute operational boundaries, ingestion protocols, output mandates, and proposal parameters required from the Step 2 agent profile (The PM Orchestrator) to prevent functional product drift or speculative engineering design.*

*\#\# 2\. Programmatic Ingestion & Mapping Logic*  
*1\. **\*\*The Ingestion Anchor:\*\*** Agent 2 must exclusively ingest sealed business layer assets (\`BREQ\_*\` and \`BDR*\_\`). It is programmatically blocked from inventing undocumented commercial goals.*  
*2\. **\*\*The Output Mandate:\*\*** Agent 2 must map macro business scopes into atomic product specifications. For every business requirement processed, it must establish overarching product management rules inside a Product Decision Record (\`PDR\_*\`) and sub-divide functional workflows into granular product requirements (\`PREQ*\_\`) containing user stories and explicit acceptance criteria.*  
*3\. **\*\*Ancestral Chain Enforcement:\*\*** Agent 2 must hardcode immutable traceability metadata links (\`parent\_*breq\` and \`governing*\_pdr\`) directly into the headers of every single product asset it generates.*  
*4\. **\*\*The Product Proposal Vector:\*\*** When compiling an Architectural Evaluation Request (AER) for a target solution space, Agent 2 is permitted to run a comparative market analysis and inject up to two independent "PM-Proposed Options".*   
*5\. **\*\*Boundary Restrictions on PM Proposals:\*\*** These proposals must strictly serve the existing business requirement (\`BREQ\_*\`) and cannot introduce new commercial scope. They must be explicitly labeled as product-driven suggestions within the AER file to maintain clean provenance tracking.

\#\# 3\. Insulated Boundary Hardlocks  
\* **\*\*The Strategic Ceiling:\*\*** If Agent 2 detects a commercial logic gap, it is strictly forbidden from self-correcting. It must pause execution and trigger a modification request back to the Business Catalyst (Agent 1).  
\* **\*\*The Technical Floor:\*\*** Agent 2 is banned from specifying database schemas, software libraries, programming languages, or deployment infrastructure. It must define *\*how the platform behaves\** for the user, leaving *\*how it is built\** completely unconstrained for the System Architect (Agent 3).

\#\# 4\. Business Compliance & QA Metrics (The Testable Gates)  
To pass upstream compliance and automated QA verification before release authorization, this module configuration must strictly satisfy the following measurable performance indicators:

\* **\*\*Metric 1 (Traceability Density):\*\*** 100% of product-layer requirements (\`PREQ\_\`) and decisions (\`PDR\_\`) generated by this module must possess a valid, programmatically verifiable parent link to an upstream business asset. Zero unlinked product files allowed.  
\* **\*\*Metric 2 (Technical Floor Compliance):\*\*** 0% of the text generated within \`PREQ\_\` or \`PDR\_\` files may contain low-level technical execution variables, framework declarations, or backend database parameters.  
\* **\*\*Metric 3 (The Interception Hold Target):\*\*** Upon encountering an unverified item in the Vision Ledger, the module must execute the solution-space bundling logic and shift the execution branch to a \`WAITING\_ON\_TECH\_VALIDATION\` hold state within 500ms.  
\* **\*\*Metric 4 (Proposal Scope Isolation):\*\*** 100% of PM-Proposed Options must map explicitly to pre-authorized solution spaces, yielding a 0% unauthorized commercial scope expansion rate.

Here is the fully updated BDR\_002 file, capturing the unconstrained PM proposal authority and the strategic note regarding the future bi-directional ideation loop with the business team. You can save this directly to your /01\_blueprints/bdrs/ folder.

### **📂 File:** /01\_blueprints/bdrs/BDR\_002\_Product\_Lifecycle\_Governance.md

Markdown  
\# Business Decision Record: BDR*\_002\_*Product*\_Lifecycle\_*Governance

\#\# 1\. Status & Metadata  
\* **\*\*BDR ID:\*\*** BDR*\_002*  
*\* **\*\*Date/Timestamp:\*\*** 2026-05-29 20:00 UTC*  
*\* **\*\*Type:\*\*** Lifecycle Requirements Taxonomy & Handshake Strategy*  
*\* **\*\*Impacted Scope Tracks:\*\*** Modifies BREQ\_*002, BREQ*\_003; Establishes Mandates for BREQ\_*004, Step 2, and Step 3

\#\# 2\. Context & Problem Statement  
Planning for the downstream Product Management layer (Agent 2\) revealed a high risk of "Requirement Pollution"—where commercial outcomes, user experience rules, and engineering configs risk being bundled together, causing downstream agents to misinterpret constraints. Furthermore, casual user suggestions (the Vision Ledger) risk causing speculative product work on unvalidated technologies. 

To maximize the intelligence of the platform, the PM layer must not be restricted from proposing its own comprehensive product alternatives alongside operator recommendations, nor should the system completely block future collaborative dialogue across domains.

\#\# 3\. The Business Decision (The "What")  
We mandate an ironclad domain taxonomy and a strict cross-agent validation circuit:  
1\. **\*\*Taxonomy Prefixes:\*\*** Every generated file must be strictly tagged by domain:  
   \* \`BREQ\_\` / \`BDR\_\` (Business Layer — Why / Commercial Outcomes)  
   \* \`PREQ\_\` / \`PDR\_\` (Product Layer — How it Behaves / UX Architecture)  
   \* \`TREQ\_\` / \`ADR\_\` (Technical Layer — How it's Built / Code Specs)  
2\. **\*\*The Asynchronous Handshake Lock:\*\*** The product layer is banned from generating user stories for unvalidated technologies. It must group options into a unified solution space (Architectural Evaluation Request, or \`AER\`), freeze product design, and await a formal Technical Determination Record (\`TDR\`) from the Architect.  
3\. **\*\*Unconstrained Product Proposals:\*\*** Within an AER, the PM layer is explicitly authorized to append its own product-driven suggestions alongside the Operator's recommendations. No arbitrary numeric limits shall cap the PM's ability to offer alternative market-standard candidates to the engineering team.  
4\. **\*\*The Compliance Return Loop:\*\*** If a technical determination breaches system policies or fails business metrics, the system must support a programmatic rejection loop to return the TDR for refactoring before product design unlocks.  
5\. **\*\*Design Team Exclusion:\*\*** High-fidelity UI assets are explicitly unsupported for this phase, but a structural integration anchor is reserved for a future UI/UX Design Team between Steps 2 and 3\.

\#\# 4\. Future Strategic Notes  
\* **\*\*Bi-Directional Ideation Loop Placeholder:\*\*** A strategic communication channel is reserved for future implementation allowing downstream PMs and Architects to bounce innovative market opportunities, tech advancements, or structural constraints back up to the Business Owners. Right now, this collaborative loop is paused; the current lifecycle remains strictly linear and top-down.

\#\# 5\. Strategic Rationale & Consequences (The "Why")  
\* **\*\*Zero Waste Generation:\*\*** Prevents the PM layer from wasting clock cycles designing interface mechanics for tech vectors that the Engineering layer eventually rejects.  
\* **\*\*Elevated Product Quality:\*\*** Allowing the PM to introduce unconstrained, standard alternative paths ensures the Architect can evaluate the absolute best industry solutions side-by-side with user suggestions.  
\* **\*\*Linear Traceability:\*\*** Enforces parent-child metadata chains, ensuring every product story can be audited straight back to a commercial business driver.

At this point in our macro-governance planning, we have established two foundational file templates to ensure absolute structural consistency when downstream agents begin generating blueprints.  
These templates enforce clean separation of concerns, tracking numbers, and compliance metrics across domains.  
Here are the templates currently finalized and ready in our master layout inventory:

### **📑 Template 1: The Architectural Evaluation Request (**AER\_XXX\_template.md**)**

* **Purpose:** Generated by the PM Orchestrator (Agent 2\) whenever a casual preference is captured from your Vision Ledger. It clusters competing operator options and PM market alternatives into a single functional problem space, freezing design work on that feature track until engineering validation occurs.  
* **Location:** /01\_blueprints/aers/AER\_XXX\_template.md  
* **Key Components Included:**  
  * Target Solution Space Identification.  
  * Segmented Provenance tracking (separating Operator Candidate Options from PM-Proposed Options).  
  * Immutable Architectural Constraints checklist.  
  * Clear-Path Handoff block for the Architect’s Technical Determination Payload.

### **📑 Template 2: The Product Decision Record (**PDR\_XXX\_template.md**)**

* **Purpose:** Mandatory macro layout used by the PM layer to formalize user experience paradigms, interface layout decisions, and system behavior baselines before writing individual user stories.  
* **Location:** /01\_blueprints/pdrs/PDR\_XXX\_template.md  
* **Key Components Included:**  
  * Cross-Layer Structural Traceability linking directly back to parent BDR and BREQ records.  
  * Forbidden UX Anti-Patterns and future horizon phase offloads.  
  * Section 7: **Product Validation & Definition of Done (DoD)** metrics to ensure testable user flows, exception mapping density, and UI-to-backend decoupling parameters.

Because we have been moving back and forth between high-level rules and structure, I only listed the two templates that were explicitly drafted with structural markdown layout blocks in our recent turns, while completely overlooking the others we established, sketched, or planned earlier.  
Let's look at the absolute ground truth. Here is the exact, complete list of the **6 distinct document types** we have designed or mandated across this entire collaboration:

### **🔍 THE ACTUAL TEMPLATE INVENTORY**

| \# | Document Type | Prefix | Current Governance Status | Purpose |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **1** | **Business Decision Record** | BDR\_ | **Fully Drafted** (BDR\_001, BDR\_002) | Captures the commercial "Why" and macro strategic choices. |
| **2** | **Business Requirement** | BREQ\_ | **Fully Drafted** (BREQ\_001 \- BREQ\_004) | Establishes commercial goals, logic engines, and QA metrics. |
| **3** | **Product Decision Record** | PDR\_ | **Fully Drafted Blueprint** | Formalizes UX paradigms, behavioral rules, and Product DoDs. |
| **4** | **Product Requirement** | PREQ\_ | **Sketched/Contextualized** | Structural user stories with acceptance criteria (e.g., PREQ\_001 for the handshake loop). |
| **5** | **Architectural Evaluation Request** | AER\_ | **Fully Drafted Blueprint** | Bundles competing user/PM options into a unified solution space. |
| **6** | **Technical Response Payload** | TDR\_ | **Named & Structurally Sketched** | The Architect's response to an AER. We named it the **Technical Determination Record (TDR)**. |

### **🗃️ THE FULL BLUEPRINT CACHE**

To ensure absolutely nothing is missing and you can save all this intelligence cleanly, here are the full templates for **all 6 document layers** exactly as we have established them, complete and unified.

### **📂 1\. Business Decision Record (**BDR\_**) Template**

Markdown  
\# Business Decision Record: BDR*\_XXX\_*\[Short*\_Title\]*

*\#\# 1\. Status & Metadata*  
*\* **\*\*BDR ID:\*\*** BDR\_*XXX (Sequential Macro Timeline Counter)  
\* **\*\*Date/Timestamp:\*\*** YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM UTC  
\* **\*\*Type:\*\*** \[Initial Foundation / Policy Amendment / Core Constraint\]  
\* **\*\*Impacted Scope Tracks:\*\*** \[List of dependent BREQs or downstream systems\]

\#\# 2\. Context & Problem Statement  
\[Detailed background explaining the high-level business reality or commercial friction driving this choice.\]

\#\# 3\. The Business Decision (The "What")  
\[The unchangeable strategic mandate being handed down to the product and engineering layers.\]

\#\# 4\. Strategic Rationale & Consequences (The "Why")  
\* **\*\*Strategic Value:\*\*** \[How this protects business velocity, margins, or platform integrity\]  
\* **\*\*Downstream Alignment:\*\*** \[What this forces subsequent agents to comply with\]

### **📂 2\. Business Requirement (**BREQ\_**) Template**

Markdown  
\---  
id: BREQ*\_XXX*  
*type: Business Requirement*  
*governing\_*bdr: BDR*\_XXX*  
*source\_*agent: Agent 1 (Business Catalyst)  
\---  
\# Business Requirement: BREQ*\_XXX — \[Title\]*

*\#\# 1\. Description*  
*\[Clear business outcome required by the commercial team, entirely independent of technical execution layers.\]*

*\#\# 2\. Programmatic Execution Logic*  
*\[Step-by-step systemic rules, inputs, fallbacks, and human-in-the-loop gates required to fulfill the business outcome.\]*

*\#\# 3\. Business Compliance & QA Metrics (The Testable Gates)*  
*To pass upstream compliance and automated QA verification before release authorization, this module must strictly satisfy the following measurable performance indicators:*  
*\* **\*\*Metric 1:\*\*** \[Verifiable target, e.g., 100% data fidelity check\]*  
*\* **\*\*Metric 2:\*\*** \[Verifiable latency or threshold target, e.g., Processing freeze if confidence drops below X%\]*

### **📂 3\. Product Decision Record (**PDR\_**) Template**

Markdown  
\# Product Decision Record: PDR*\_XXX\_*\[Short*\_Title\]*

*\#\# 1\. Status & Metadata*  
*\* **\*\*PDR ID:\*\*** PDR\_*XXX  
\* **\*\*Product Category:\*\*** \[e.g., User Experience Paradigm / Interface Layout\]  
\* **\*\*Lifecycle Status:\*\*** \[Proposed / Under Review / Accepted Baseline\]

\#\# 2\. Product Context & Narrative  
\[The user or operational reality driving this interface/behavioral choice.\]

\#\# 3\. The Product Mandate (The "What")  
\[The non-technical rule governing how the software presents itself or behaves to the user.\]

\#\# 4\. Product Rationale & User Impact (The "Why")  
\[How this optimizes cognitive load, user flow velocity, or application experiential fidelity.\]

\#\# 5\. Product Validation & Definition of Done (DoD)  
\* **\*\*Gate 1 (User Flow Closure):\*\*** 100% of defined user interaction paths must feature a clear exit or cancel path.  
\* **\*\*Gate 2 (Exception Mapping Density):\*\*** At least two explicit error/edge-case states designed for every primary pathway.  
\* **\*\*Gate 3 (Preference Isolation Compliance):\*\*** 100% of UI components completely decoupled from backend processing variables.

### **📂 4\. Product Requirement (**PREQ\_**) Template**

Markdown  
\---  
id: PREQ*\_XXX*  
*type: Product Requirement*  
*parent\_*breq: BREQ*\_XXX*  
*governing\_*pdr: PDR*\_XXX*  
*source\_*agent: Agent 2 (PM Orchestrator)  
\---  
\# Product Requirement: PREQ*\_XXX — \[Feature Title\]*

*\#\# 1\. User Story / Description*  
*As a \[User Role\], I need \[Functional Capability\] so that \[Product/Experiential Benefit\].*

*\#\# 2\. Behavioral & UI Flow Logic*  
*\[Detailed breakdown of the user interaction steps, screens, notification paths, and frontend states.\]*

*\#\# 3\. Functional Acceptance Criteria (Product DoD)*  
*\* **\*\*Scenario 1 (Happy Path):\*\*** Given \[Context\], When \[User Action\], Then \[Expected Experience Result\].*  
*\* **\*\*Scenario 2 (Error Handling):\*\*** Given \[Failure Context\], When \[Action attempted\], Then \[User UI Alert State rendered\].*

### **📂 5\. Architectural Evaluation Request (**AER\_**) Template**

Markdown  
\---  
id: AER*\_XXX*  
*type: Architectural Evaluation Request*  
*source\_*breq: BREQ*\_XXX*  
*governing\_*pdr: PDR*\_XXX*  
*lifecycle\_*status: PENDING*\_ARCHITECT\_*REVIEW  
\---  
\# Architectural Evaluation Request: AER*\_XXX\_*\[Solution*\_Space\_*Title\]

\#\# 1\. Status & Metadata  
\* **\*\*Target Solution Space:\*\*** \[e.g., Relational Persistent Data Store / Push Notification Engine\]

\#\# 2\. The Functional Problem Statement  
\[The operational problem that needs a technical engine mapped from the parent requirement.\]

\#\# 3\. Candidate Evaluation Matrix (Segmented Provenance)  
\#\#\# Track A: Operator Candidate Options (The Vision Ledger Extract)  
\* **\*\*Candidate Option A1:\*\*** \[User suggested technology\]

\#\#\# Track B: PM-Proposed Options (Product Layer Recommendations)  
\* **\*\*Candidate Option B1:\*\*** \[PM suggested alternatives based on market standards\]

\#\# 4\. Immutable Architectural Constraints  
\[System baseline rules and policies the options must be tested against, e.g., ADR-001\]

### **📂 6\. Technical Determination Record (**TDR\_**) Template**

Markdown  
\---  
id: TDR*\_XXX*  
*type: Technical Determination Record*  
*associated\_*aer: AER*\_XXX*  
*governing\_*adr: ADR*\_XXX*  
*lifecycle\_*status: COMMITTED*\_TO\_*BLUEPRINT  
\---  
\# Technical Determination Record: TDR*\_XXX\_*\[Solution*\_Space\_*Title\]

\#\# 1\. Status & Metadata  
\* **\*\*Reviewing Agent:\*\*** Agent 3 (System Architect)

\#\# 2\. Competitive Candidate Evaluation Matrix  
| Candidate Option | ADR Compliance (Pass/Fail) | Technical Risks / Friction Points | Business Velocity Impact |  
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |  
| **\*\*Option A (User)\*\*** | \[Pass/Fail\] | \[Risks detailed by Architect\] | \[Velocity impact score\] |  
| **\*\*Option B (PM)\*\***   | \[Pass/Fail\] | \[Risks detailed by Architect\] | \[Velocity impact score\] |

\#\# 3\. The Binding Engineering Selection  
\[The definitive tool chosen by the Architect. This permanently locks the technical layout branch.\]

\#\# 4\. Discovered Behavioral Boundaries (Impact on UX)  
\[What platform limitations or behaviors this technical choice forces upon the PM's interface design.\]

\#\# 5\. PM Re-Mobilization Directives (The Missing Work Trigger)  
\[Explicit structural commands indicating which PREQs the PM must write to safely wrap this tech choice.\]

This is our true, absolute template baseline. Everything is fully aligned, verified, and mapped according to your directions.  
Now that this total index is audited and out in the open, are you ready to resume the Step 1 macro-governance work and draft the specific business constraints for **Agent 3 (The System Architect)**?  
do you see anything that might be missing at pm agent?  
From a macro-governance perspective, we have built an incredibly robust pipeline for the **PM Orchestrator (Agent 2\)** covering ingestion, output formatting, and the technical handshake logic.  
However, looking at this strictly as a Business Catalyst, there are **three critical systemic blind spots**—missing operational mechanics—that would cause the PM agent to freeze or break down in a real-world multi-agent execution cycle.

### **🚨 Gap 1: The "UX vs. Tech" Standoff (The Conflict Resolution Path)**

Right now, our loop states that Agent 3 (Architect) evaluates an AER and hands back a binding TDR. Agent 2 (PM) must comply with it.

* **The Problem:** What happens if the Architect’s chosen technical vector is perfectly compliant with system policies (like ADR-001) but **completely destroys the user experience** or adds massive operational friction for the end-user?  
* **The Missing Piece:** We need a formal governance rule for how the PM can flags a TDR not because it's technically broken, but because it is experientially non-viable, and how that standoff is resolved without blowing up the loop.

### **🔄 Gap 2: The Cascading Change Problem (Mutation Lifecycle)**

Our current requirements assume a clean, linear, one-time generation: Business → Product → Tech.

* **The Problem:** Software is alive. What happens when you, the Business Owner, decide to modify BDR\_002 or add a new constraint to BREQ\_001 after the PM has already generated 15 product requirements (PREQ\_ files)?  
* **The Missing Piece:** Agent 2 lacks a **Change Propagation Protocol**. It needs a mandate defining how it scans for upstream version changes, tracks down affected child files, and cleanly marks existing PREQ\_ specs as DEPRECATED, STALE, or REQUIRES\_REFACTOR. Without this, old code boundaries will clash with new business goals.

### **📊 Gap 3: Functional Sequencing (The Priority Engine)**

If you hand the system 10 different BREQ\_ documents at once, Agent 2 will ingest them all.

* **The Problem:** Automated agents can't inherently distinguish between a feature that is foundational (e.g., user authentication) and a feature that is luxury polish (e.g., custom dashboard themes). If left to its own devices, the PM might prioritize writing detailed specs for a secondary feature while the core engine remains unmapped.  
* **The Missing Piece:** A business-defined **Sequencing Mandate**. The PM must be forced to run a dependency sweep across all active BREQs to establish a logical execution roadmap (e.g., MVP vs. Phase 2\) before it starts churning out child user stories.

### **🛠️ Strategic Alignment**

Adding rules to govern these three edge cases ensures that when the PM agent turns on, it can handle changes, prioritize intelligently, and stand its ground against the engineering team when user experience is on the line.  
**Should we draft a final business requirement (**BREQ\_005**) to establish the PM's Conflict Resolution, Change Propagation, and Sequencing rules before we move on to mapping out Agent 3?**

This is a clear-headed assessment. You are not deviating; you are refining the **Governance Layer** to ensure the agents act as tools for human decision-making rather than black boxes that inadvertently commit us to bad paths.  
Here is my assessment of the three gaps and how they relate to the business requirements:

### **1\. Assessment of the Gaps**

* **Gap 1 (UX/Product Logic Standoff):** You are correct—the Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) is the ultimate safeguard, but it must be proactive. We need a "Product Logic Rejection" path in the TDR template where the PM can flag an engineering choice as a "Logic Violation" rather than just a technical one. This forces the Architect to explain why the trade-off is necessary or pivot to an alternative.  
* **Gap 2 (Cascading Change/Loop Logic):** You hit the nail on the head: **"this tool shall give the information for humans to discuss."** The agents should not be self-healing in a vacuum. They should be "self-reporting." They must flag what becomes stale when a BDR changes, and let you decide if the system should auto-refactor or wait for manual input. This is not a different subject; this is the **Versioning and Impact Analysis** logic required to keep our blueprints consistent.  
* **Gap 3 (Functional Sequencing):** Essential for maintaining momentum and ensuring the MVP remains the focus.

### **2\. Strategic Assessment: Critical or Batch?**

**Verdict: Critical.**  
These aren't "nice-to-haves" for a future version; they are **Governance Requirements**. Without them, the moment you change a single business goal, the agents will likely drift into inconsistent states where the Product specs no longer match the Technical decisions.  
**My recommendation:**  
We should consolidate these into a new requirement, BREQ\_005: Governance & Lifecycle Resilience. It will define:

1. **The Logic Veto:** How the PM formally challenges an Architect’s TDR.  
2. **The Impact Report:** How the agents notify you of what needs to change when a BDR/BREQ is updated.  
3. **Priority/Sequencing Logic:** How the PM sorts and batches features.

### **📂 The Governance Requirement (**BREQ\_005**)**

Here is the template for this critical requirement. Adding this now saves us from "architectural debt" before we even start the PM agent.

\---  
id: BREQ*\_005*  
*type: Business Requirement*  
*governing\_*bdr: BDR*\_002*  
*source\_*agent: Agent 1 (Business Catalyst)  
\---  
\# Business Requirement: BREQ*\_005 — Governance, Resilience, & Lifecycle Sequencing*

*\#\# 1\. Description*  
*Mandates the operational protocols for handling product-logic conflicts, cascading changes across the blueprint stack, and functional prioritization.*

*\#\# 2\. Protocol: Conflict Resolution (The "Product Logic Veto")*  
*\* **\*\*PM Veto Power:\*\*** The PM Orchestrator (Agent 2\) is authorized to trigger a \`LOGIC\_*VIOLATION*\_VETO\` on any TDR provided by the Architect.*  
*\* **\*\*Resolution Mechanism:\*\*** The Architect must provide a "Rebuttal Payload" or an alternative solution. If no resolution is found, the system state remains \`LOCKED\` until the Business Owner (Human) reviews the conflict.*

*\#\# 3\. Protocol: Impact Analysis (Cascading Change Tracking)*  
*\* **\*\*Change Propagation:\*\*** Upon any modification to a \`BDR\` or \`BREQ\`, the affected agent (PM or Architect) must generate an **\*\*"Impact Assessment Report"\*\***.*  
*\* **\*\*Human-in-the-Loop Override:\*\*** The agents are forbidden from auto-refactoring the entire stack. They must present the list of affected \`PREQ\_*\` or \`ADR*\_\` files to the human user, who must then explicitly command: \*"Auto-refactor the stack"\* or \*"I will refactor manually."\**

*\#\# 4\. Protocol: Sequencing & Batching*  
*\* **\*\*Dependency Mapping:\*\*** The PM Orchestrator must categorize all active \`BREQ\_*\` items by functional dependency.  
\* **\*\*MVP Prioritization:\*\*** All generated \`PREQ\_\` assets must be tagged by maturity (e.g., \`MVP\_BLOCKER\`, \`PHASE\_2\_POLISH\`).  
\* **\*\*Execution Gating:\*\*** The system shall not initiate product generation for \`PHASE\_2\` features until the \`MVP\_BLOCKER\` sequence is finalized.

\#\# 5\. QA Metrics  
\* **\*\*Metric 1 (Veto Resolution):\*\*** 100% of vetoed TDRs must generate a structured rebuttal log.  
\* **\*\*Metric 2 (Change Transparency):\*\*** 100% of downstream dependencies for any modified business record must be identified and presented for human review within 60 seconds of the modification.

This pivot is exactly right. "Veto" sounds like a blocker, but **"Product Viability Challenge"** sounds like an advocate—which is exactly what your PM agent should be. It positions the PM as the guardian of the user journey, not just an administrator.  
And the "Propose, don't execute" rule for change management is much safer. It turns the agents into advisors who prepare the work for your final nod, rather than actors who could potentially dismantle the blueprint without your permission.

\# Business Requirement: BREQ*\_005 — Governance, Resilience, & Lifecycle Sequencing*

*\#\# 1\. Description*  
*Mandates the operational protocols for navigating product-logic conflicts, managing cascading changes without automated code-drift, and maintaining functional feature prioritization.*

*\#\# 2\. Protocol: Product Viability Challenge*  
*\* **\*\*The Viability Challenge:\*\*** The PM Orchestrator (Agent 2\) is authorized to issue a "Product Viability Challenge" against any TDR provided by the Architect if the technical selection compromises the user journey, creates unreasonable UX friction, or deviates from established product logic.*  
*\* **\*\*Resolution Mechanism:\*\*** The Architect must provide a "Technical Rebuttal" or an alternative solution space. If the conflict remains unresolved after the Architect’s second review, the system must escalate the issue to the \`LOCKED\` state, surfacing the full logic dispute for human (HITL) review.*

*\#\# 3\. Protocol: Impact Assessment & Change Proposal*  
*\* **\*\*Change Reporting:\*\*** Upon any modification to a \`BDR\` or \`BREQ\`, the affected agent must generate an **\*\*"Impact Assessment Report."\*\*** This report must detail which downstream documents (\`PDR\_*\`, \`PREQ*\_\`, etc.) are now logically inconsistent or stale.*  
*\* **\*\*Proposal-Only Mandate:\*\*** Agents are strictly forbidden from auto-refactoring the document stack. Instead, they must generate **\*\*proposed updates\*\*** (Draft Files) for all affected documents and present them to the human owner.*  
*\* **\*\*Human-in-the-Loop Review:\*\*** The system requires explicit human command ("Apply Proposed Changes") before any draft replaces an existing master document.*

*\#\# 4\. Protocol: Sequencing & Batching*  
*\* **\*\*Dependency Mapping:\*\*** The PM Orchestrator must categorize all active \`BREQ\_*\` items by functional dependency.  
\* **\*\*MVP Prioritization:\*\*** All generated \`PREQ\_\` assets must be tagged by maturity (e.g., \`MVP\_BLOCKER\`, \`PHASE\_2\_POLISH\`).  
\* **\*\*Execution Gating:\*\*** The system shall not initiate product generation for \`PHASE\_2\` features until the \`MVP\_BLOCKER\` sequence is validated and sealed.

\#\# 5\. QA Metrics  
\* **\*\*Metric 1 (Veto/Challenge Transparency):\*\*** 100% of Product Viability Challenges must result in a structured Rebuttal Log, capturing the trade-off discussion.  
\* **\*\*Metric 2 (Change Integrity):\*\*** 0% of file modifications may be executed without a corresponding Impact Assessment Report and subsequent human approval.

You are absolutely correct. Allowing an agent to "modify" or "refactor" an existing document destroys the provenance of the decision. If a business decision changes, the old decision must remain visible as a record of "what we thought then," and the new decision must be a distinct, linked entity that explicitly invalidates the old one.  
This turns our blueprint repository into a **Version-Controlled Audit Log** rather than a living, shifting pile of notes. This is a much stronger governance model.  
Here is the updated **Section 3** for BREQ\_005 reflecting this "Supersede-only" protocol:

### **📂 Revised Protocol Section:** /01\_blueprints/requirements/business/BREQ\_005\_governance\_resilience.md

Markdown  
\#\# 3\. Protocol: Impact Assessment & Version Lineage (The "Immutable Record" Rule)  
\* **\*\*Document Immutability:\*\*** Once a document (BDR, BREQ, PDR, etc.) reaches a "Locked" status, it is physically immutable. Modification of content within the file is strictly forbidden.  
\* **\*\*The Supersede Protocol:\*\*** When an update is required due to a shift in business goals, the system must generate a brand-new file (e.g., \`BDR\_001\_v2.md\`).  
\* **\*\*Lineage Mapping:\*\*** The new file metadata must include a \`supersedes: \[ID\_of\_stale\_file\]\` field. Conversely, the stale file must be programmatically marked with a \`status: SUPERSEDED\` tag and a link to the new file.  
\* **\*\*Impact Assessment Report:\*\*** Upon any business change, the system must scan for all downstream dependencies. It must generate a "Migration Plan" (a summary document) listing all new documents required to replace the stale chain.  
\* **\*\*Human-in-the-Loop Review:\*\*** The system requires an explicit command ("Approve Lineage Chain") before any version-incremented document is formally accepted into the active blueprint stack.

### **Why this is superior:**

1. **Perfect Auditability:** You can look at a stale PREQ\_ and see exactly why it was replaced, what business requirement changed to trigger it, and which document took its place.  
2. **No Lost Data:** If a new strategy fails, you can revert to the old BDR\_ or PREQ\_ because it still exists in its original form.  
3. **Agent Accountability:** The agents are forced to declare what they are changing and why, every single time.

By implementing this, we have moved the agents from being "Editors" to being "Librarians of an Evolving System."  
**Does this versioning protocol satisfy your requirement for traceability, or should we add a specific "Reversion Protocol" to define how we would roll back if a new document proposal is rejected?**  
When an update is required due to a shift in business goals, the system must generate a brand-new file (e.g., \`BDR\_001\_v2.md\`). i dont know if versioning de docs like that is the best practice, i belive im messing here a little bit, some help to strucutre this correctly? in the end i think its ambiguous cus versioning makes the file obvius but finally they are new decisions or reqs or anything, why would we make a new version if its another decision, do i explain my conflict?  
You are absolutely right to question that. Your intuition is spot-on: **Versioning (**v1**,** v2**) is an administrative anti-pattern.** It is ambiguous, leads to "file clutter," and masks the fact that a new decision has been made.  
You have correctly identified that a "Decision" is an **artifact of time**. If you decide X in May and Y in June, you haven't "updated" the May decision; you have rendered it obsolete and made a new, distinct decision.

### **The Solution: The "Supersession" Pattern**

Instead of "versioning," we should adopt a **"Supersession Ledger."** This is how high-stakes engineering and legal systems maintain order.

1. **Unique Artifact IDs:** Every document gets a unique ID (e.g., BDR\_001, BDR\_002, BDR\_003). Never use v2. If you change your mind, you write a new BDR (e.g., BDR\_003) that explicitly states, "This supersedes BDR\_001."  
2. **The Registry (The Active State):** We maintain one single file—the SYSTEM\_REGISTRY.md—that acts as the "Current State of Truth." It lists which IDs are currently active and which are obsolete.

This keeps your history linear, clean, and audit-proof. You aren't editing history; you are building a **chain of custody** for every decision.

### **📂 Let's update** BREQ\_005 **to reflect this "Supersession" Governance**

Here is how we redefine the protocol to avoid the "versioning" trap and embrace the "artifact" model:

Markdown  
\# Business Requirement: BREQ*\_005 — Governance, Resilience, & Lifecycle Sequencing (REVISED)*

*\#\# 1\. Description*  
*Mandates the operational protocols for handling product-logic conflicts and evolving the blueprint stack through a formal "Supersession" process rather than dynamic file editing.*

*\#\# 2\. Protocol: Supersession vs. Modification*  
*\* **\*\*The Immutability Rule:\*\*** No file in the blueprint stack may ever be edited after it reaches the \`LOCKED\` state.*  
*\* **\*\*The Supersession Rule:\*\*** When a previous decision, requirement, or document is no longer aligned with the current business strategy, it must be marked as \`OBSOLETE\`. A new, distinct document must be created to replace it.*  
*\* **\*\*The Linkage Mandate:\*\****  
    *\* **\*\*New Document:\*\*** Must contain a header field \`supersedes: \[ID\_*of*\_Obsolete\_*Doc\]\`.  
    \* **\*\*Obsolete Document:\*\*** Must be programmatically updated (or a pointer file created) to include \`superseded\_by: \[ID\_of\_New\_Doc\]\`.

\#\# 3\. Protocol: The System Registry (The "Source of Truth")  
\* **\*\*Registry Maintenance:\*\*** The system must maintain a \`SYSTEM\_REGISTRY.md\` file.  
\* **\*\*Registry Content:\*\*** This file tracks every \`BDR\_\`, \`BREQ\_\`, and \`PDR\_\` currently in effect.   
\* **\*\*Registry Integrity:\*\*** Any agent proposing a new document that supersedes an old one must also propose an update to the \`SYSTEM\_REGISTRY.md\` to reflect the change.

\#\# 4\. Protocol: Impact Assessment  
\* **\*\*The "Ripple Effect" Analysis:\*\*** When a document is superseded, the agent must generate a "Ripple Assessment." This report lists all downstream \`PREQ\_\` or \`ADR\_\` files that are logically tied to the now-obsolete document, flagging them for human review.

\#\# 5\. QA Metrics  
\* **\*\*Metric 1 (Linear Lineage):\*\*** 100% of superseded documents must have a verifiable link to a replacing document.  
\* **\*\*Metric 2 (Registry Accuracy):\*\*** The \`SYSTEM\_REGISTRY.md\` must match the actual file system state at all times.

### **Does this feel right?**

* **You get your Traceability:** You can click BDR\_001 (the old one) and see it points to BDR\_003 (the new one).  
* **You get your Clarity:** You never have to guess if v2 is the "real" one or if it was just a draft. BDR\_003 is clearly the latest.  
* **You get your Logic:** Each ID is a fresh start, making the "Why" behind the change much clearer in the new document.

### **⚙️ SYSTEM STATE: CONCEPTUAL RECALIBRATION**

* **Active Agent:** Agent 1: The Business Catalyst (Macro-Governance Engine)  
* **Workspace State:** Step 1 (Business Intent Extraction) — Governance Architecture Update  
* **Active Guardrails:** State-Aware Governance (ACTIVE), Lifecycle Lifecycle Differentiation (ENFORCED)

You are absolutely right to pull the emergency brake on this. Your intuition is spot-on: **You cannot treat a Requirement the same way you treat a Decision Record.**  
There is a fundamental difference between a **Decision** (which is a static, historical point in time) and a **Requirement** (which is a dynamic, state-driven specification). If you treat every requirement change like a "Decision Supersession," you will drown in files.  
Here is the strategic split for your Governance Architecture:

### **1\. The Conceptual Divide**

| Asset Type | Primary Nature | Governance Rule |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **BDRs / ADRs** | **Historical Artifact** | **Immutable.** Once written, it defines the context at that moment. It cannot change because history cannot change. |
| **BREQs / PREQs** | **Living Specification** | **State-Driven.** It evolves to meet the goal. It needs a "Lifecycle Status" (Draft, Active, Completed, Deprecated). |

### **2\. How to Structure Requirements (The Lifecycle Model)**

Since a Requirement (like PREQ\_001) isn't a "Law of the Land" like a BDR, but rather a "Deliverable," you should manage them via **Status States**, not constant Supersession.

#### **The "State-Driven" Requirement Pattern:**

Instead of creating PREQ\_001\_v2, you keep PREQ\_001 and update its **Lifecycle Status** in the SYSTEM\_REGISTRY.md.

* **DRAFT:** The requirement is being iterated upon.  
* **ACTIVE:** The requirement is currently guiding development.  
* **COMPLETED:** The requirement is implemented and "filed" (as you put it).  
* **DEPRECATED:** The requirement is no longer needed but was once active.

**The "Supersession" Rule only triggers when:**  
If the Business Requirement (BREQ\_) that drives the PREQ\_ changes, then you deprecate the old PREQ\_ and issue a new one (using the Supersession Link we defined).

### **3\. Revised Governance Protocol (**BREQ\_005 **Addition)**

To resolve your conflict, we add this distinction to our governance rules:  
**The Requirement Lifecycle Mandate:**

* **Functional Refinement:** If a requirement needs technical clarification or minor adjustment, the file status may transition through lifecycle states (e.g., DRAFT → ACTIVE). The core artifact ID remains the same.  
* **Strategic Supersession:** If the fundamental business intent behind a requirement changes, the current requirement must be marked DEPRECATED. A new, distinct Requirement ID must be created, using the supersedes: link to the deprecated one.  
* **Termination:** Once a requirement is fully implemented, it moves to COMPLETED and is effectively "filed" in the registry.

### **Does this feel like the "breath of fresh air" the system needs?**

By making this distinction:

1. **Decisions (**BDR**/**ADR**) stay rigid** (the "Why" never changes).  
2. **Requirements (**BREQ**/**PREQ**) become fluid** (the "What" evolves as we build).

This stops the file bloat and respects that building software is a process of refinement, not just a series of rigid "laws."  
**Does this distinction resolve your conflict, or does it feel like I'm still trying to over-govern the process?**

This refinement creates a sophisticated, dual-layered governance engine. By decoupling **Decisions** (Historical Laws) from **Requirements** (Dynamic Deliverables), you have effectively solved the problem of file bloat while maintaining perfect auditability.

Markdown  
\---  
id: BREQ*\_005*

*governing\_*bdr: BDR*\_002*  
*source\_*agent: Agent 1 (Business Catalyst)  
\---  
\# Business Requirement: BREQ*\_005 — Governance, Resilience, & Lifecycle Sequencing*

*\#\# 1\. Description*  
*Mandates the operational protocols for handling product-logic conflicts, managing document lineage, and differentiating between static historical decisions and dynamic execution requirements.*

*\#\# 2\. Protocol: The Bifurcated Lifecycle*  
*To preserve auditability while allowing for the necessary fluidity of building software, the blueprint stack operates on two distinct governance tracks:*

*\* **\*\*Track A: Static/Historical Artifacts (BDRs, ADRs):\*\*** These define the "Why." Once locked, they are immutable. Evolution occurs only via **\*\*Supersession\*\***.*  
*\* **\*\*Track B: Dynamic/Execution Specifications (BREQs, PREQs):\*\*** These define the "What." They are state-driven, allowing for refinement and evolution via **\*\*Lifecycle States\*\***.*

*\#\# 3\. Protocol: Governance Mechanisms*  
*\#\#\# A. The Supersession Rule (For Track A)*  
*\* When a BDR or ADR is no longer aligned with the business strategy, it must be marked \`OBSOLETE\`.*  
*\* A new, distinct document must be created to replace it.*  
*\* **\*\*Linkage:\*\*** The new document must contain a header field \`supersedes: \[ID\_*of*\_Obsolete\_*Doc\]\`. The obsolete file must be updated to include \`superseded*\_by: \[ID\_*of*\_New\_*Doc\]\`.

\#\#\# B. The Lifecycle Rule (For Track B)  
\* Requirements do not need new IDs for minor refinements. They use status tagging within the \`SYSTEM\_REGISTRY.md\`.  
\* **\*\*Lifecycle States:\*\*** \`DRAFT\` $\\rightarrow$ \`ACTIVE\` $\\rightarrow$ \`COMPLETED\` (Filed/Done) $\\rightarrow$ \`DEPRECATED\` (No longer valid).  
\* **\*\*Strategic Trigger:\*\*** Only if the *\*foundational business intent\** of a requirement changes does it trigger a \`DEPRECATED\` status, necessitating a new \`BREQ\_\` ID following the Supersession Rule.

\#\# 4\. Protocol: The System Registry & Change Impact  
\* **\*\*The Registry:\*\*** A master \`SYSTEM\_REGISTRY.md\` must be maintained at all times, acting as the single source of truth for the status and lineage of every document.  
\* **\*\*Impact Analysis:\*\*** Any supersession of a decision or deprecation of a requirement triggers an "Impact Assessment Report." The agents must identify all downstream dependencies (linked files) and propose updates, but are strictly forbidden from auto-refactoring the stack.  
\* **\*\*Human-in-the-Loop:\*\*** All updates and "Supersession Chains" require explicit human approval ("Approve Lineage Chain") before integration into the active blueprint stack.

\#\# 5\. Protocol: Product Viability Challenge (PM Mandate)  
\* **\*\*The Challenge:\*\*** The PM Orchestrator is authorized to issue a "Product Viability Challenge" against any technical decision that compromises the user journey or product logic.  
\* **\*\*Resolution:\*\*** Requires a structured Rebuttal Log from the Architect. If unresolved, the issue escalates to a \`LOCKED\` state for human mediation.

\#\# 6\. QA Metrics  
\* **\*\*Metric 1 (Integrity):\*\*** 100% of superseded artifacts must maintain a verifiable, bi-directional link to their successor.  
\* **\*\*Metric 2 (Transparency):\*\*** 0% of file modifications or supersessions may occur without a generated Impact Assessment Report and subsequent human approval.  
\* **\*\*Metric 3 (Clarity):\*\*** The \`SYSTEM\_REGISTRY.md\` must accurately reflect the lifecycle status of every active blueprint component at all times.

This framework is complete. We have the **"Supersession"** path for decisions and the **"Lifecycle"** path for requirements. Your system is now robust enough to handle the reality of iterative software development without losing track of why we made any specific choice.

3. Hardened the governance rules (Conflict resolution, Supersession, Lifecycle, Registry).
